finding: resolve_pane OR-matches exact-path and basename in one find(), so a basename-only match shadows a later exact-path match; two open docs sharing a filename route non-deterministically to the wrong pane (read/comment/track-changes hit the wrong document).
sole-pane fallback routes to the single connected pane regardless of doc_key match, so an op on doc A while only doc B's pane is open silently reads/mutates B; the pane never validates the target.

route_response resolves PENDING by global corr id alone, not bound to the originating pane; corr ids are a predictable sequential AtomicU64, so a second/compromised pane can forge a reply for another pane's request and win the take-once race (cross-pane response spoofing / confused deputy).

on socket close, unregister_pane drops the PANES entry but not the PENDING oneshots for that pane, so an in-flight call_pane hangs the full 15s timeout instead of fast-failing; the broker's 'socket closed mid-flight' arm is effectively unreachable/dead.

opReadDocument (Excel used-range TSV and Word body.text) has no size cap: a large sheet/doc materializes an unbounded string in the WebView, ships it over WSS, and feeds an unbounded LLM tool result — memory/DoS.

classify_pane_frame register handler stores pane-supplied host+doc_key with no validation or length bound; a compromised pane can register an arbitrary/over-long doc_key to influence routing (doc-identity spoofing).

the daemon sends doc_id (target doc_full_name) in every request but the Office.js op handlers ignore it and act on whatever document the pane is attached to, removing the only pane-side check that could catch broker mis-routing.

no test registers two live answering panes and asserts each call's reply routes to the correct waiter (positive multi-pane routing) — exactly the interaction most likely broken by route_response corr keying + resolve_pane ambiguity.

run_mock_pane hand-reimplements the pane wire contract rather than driving the real taskpane.js; the actual taskpane.js dispatch/reply/op handlers are covered by no automated test (only the live checklists), so an envelope bug in taskpane.js passes every test.

get_tracked_changes is not in the native PPT pre-gate group, so on a PowerPoint pane it round-trips and returns OFFICE_PANE_ERROR ('only supported in Word') instead of the consistent OFFICE_UNSUPPORTED_ON_HOST the other Word-only ops return.

call_pane collapses every pane JSON-RPC error into OFFICE_PANE_ERROR, discarding the pane's distinct codes (-32002 unsupported-host, -32003 anchor-not-found, -32601 unknown-method); the daemon never differentiates the vocabulary the pane defines.

BridgeResponse.jsonrpc is a required field (no serde default), so a reply omitting jsonrpc fails from_value and is silently dropped, hanging the caller to the 15s timeout instead of a clear protocol error; the drop is also unlogged.
call_pane does not enforce result-XOR-error: a reply with neither yields Ok(Null); a reply with both silently prefers error. A malformed pane reply is coerced rather than rejected.
OFFICE_PANE_TIMEOUT message states the failure but, unlike OFFICE_PANE_NOT_CONNECTED, offers no remedy/next step (retry, large-document note) — less actionable for the model.

TEST-8 asserts only that a later round-trip succeeds after the junk/stale frames; it never asserts the stale-id (999999) response failed to resolve a pending call, so the stale-id handling could be removed and the test still pass.
the 15s CALL_TIMEOUT can be too short for a legitimate large-document Word.run/Excel.run sync, surfacing a spurious OFFICE_PANE_TIMEOUT; compounded by the uncapped read.
call_pane_with_timeout clones every registered pane's doc_key into a Vec per call to resolve one pane; needless per-request allocation (fine at current pane counts).

the daemon→pane sink is an unbounded mpsc and the select! outbound arm awaits socket.send inline; a stuck pane TCP send parks handle_socket (inbound unread) while concurrent call_pane calls enqueue unboundedly.

session_token is documented as frame-level auth but is never verified on inbound frames — only the WSS upgrade validates a token. Acceptable for a single-user desktop daemon, but the advertised frame-level auth is not enforced.
no explicit cap on registered panes (PANES) or in-flight requests (PENDING); bounded only by concurrent sockets / the timeout cleanup, with no hard ceiling.
OFFICE_PANE_REQUIRED is removed and replaced by OFFICE_PANE_NOT_CONNECTED/TIMEOUT/ERROR; a consumer keyed on the old code breaks. Low impact — it was an explicit not-yet-wired placeholder.
route_response silently drops unknown/stale corr ids and the tx send error; consistent with the auth.rs reference but lacks the elicitation registry's tracing on the drop path.
